messages.text: 你看一下有一个最新的pr  是关于提交到github的  关于 ssh 和http的  你看看有没有
我先按仓库里的工作约定把上下文补齐，再去查这个仓库最近的 PR 和相关提交说明，重点看有没有提到 GitHub 提交时的 `SSH` / `HTTP`。
我先把仓库记忆里的当前目标和待办读一下，避免偏离这个仓库的工作约束；同时我已经看到远端目前配置的是 `HTTPS`，不是 `SSH`。
我现在直接查 GitHub 上这个仓库最新的 PR 列表和详情，看看最近有没有标题、描述或改动里提到 `SSH` / `HTTP` 的。
最新 PR #38 我已经打开确认了，内容是 README 里关于 `raw upload defaults` 的说明，不是 SSH/HTTP。接着我把仓库里所有 PR 标题/正文和代码文档里一起搜一遍，避免漏掉更早的相关改动。
全量正文检索那一步的 GraphQL 查询出了个参数转义问题，我改用 GitHub CLI 的 PR 搜索接口继续查，结果会更直接一些。
目前已经有一个比较明确的信号了：GitHub 上 PR 搜索里 `ssh` 是空结果，`http/https` 命中的几个 PR 看起来更像是测试或文档里的 URL 字样，不像是在处理 GitHub 提交协议。我再把这几个命中的 PR 详情和本地 Git 配置一起核对一下。
我找到一个确实跟“推到 GitHub”相关的 PR 了，但它不是最新的，是更早的 `#15`，里面加了 GitHub Pages deploy。现在我看一下它到底有没有处理 `SSH` / `HTTP(S)` 协议切换。
核心结论已经比较清楚了：`最新 PR` 没有这类内容；唯一明显跟“推到 GitHub”有关的是较早的 `#15`，而且它的实现是“沿用当前 `origin` 的 URL”，不是专门做 `SSH` / `HTTP` 协议判断或切换。我把对应代码行号取出来，方便你直接核对。
顺带把仓库约定里的 MemoryTree 提醒补一句：这个项目有一条未清告警，和本仓库相关的是 `2026-03-20` 起的 heartbeat `push_failed`，另外还有多条 transcript 的 `sensitive_match` 提示；这次我先没动它们，只专注在 PR 检查。
我把这次会话记一条简短 chat log 到项目记忆里，保持这个仓库的 MemoryTree 记录连续，然后给你一个结论。
我现在只做一个很小的记忆更新：新增今天这次问询的 chat log，不改目标和 todo。

看了，截至 `2026-03-25`，这个仓库最新的 GitHub PR 是 [#38](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/pull/38)，标题是 `docs(readme): clarify quick-start raw upload defaults`，创建于 `2026-03-24`。这个 PR 跟 GitHub 提交、`SSH`、`HTTP/HTTPS` 都没关系。

我又往前查了一层，结论是：
- 搜 `ssh` 没有命中任何 PR。
- 搜 `http/https` 只命中了 [#28](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/pull/28)、[#20](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/pull/20)、[#15](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/pull/15)。其中前两个只是正文或测试里带 URL，不是处理 GitHub 提交协议。
- 真正和“推到 GitHub”有关的是较早的 [#15](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/pull/15)，但它也不是在做 `SSH`/`HTTP` 切换。当前实现只是复用已有 `origin` 地址：先读 `origin`，再按同一个地址去 push，见 [github-pages.ts](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/src/report/deploy/github-pages.ts#L53) 和 [github-pages.ts](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/src/report/deploy/github-pages.ts#L90)。
- 你这个本地仓库现在的 `origin` 是 `https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow.git`，没有单独的 `pushurl`，所以当前本地推送走的是 `HTTPS`，不是 `SSH`。

另外我按仓库约定补了一条会话记录：[2026-03-25_12-22.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/Memory/03_chat_logs/2026-03-25_12-22.md)。如果你要，我下一步可以直接帮你把这个仓库的 remote 改成 SSH，并顺手验证 push 用的是哪条链路。
就是今天提交的pr
我再按当前时间重新核一次“今天”的定义，直接对照 `2026-03-25` 本地时区和 GitHub 的时间戳，避免我们把昨天 UTC 的 PR 误当成今天。
我把时间转换那一步修一下，再给你一个明确到日期和时区的结论。
时间偏移格式在 PowerShell 里有点挑，我直接用 `UTC+8` 算。

https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/pull/39   这个pr  你看看什么情况呢  有价值吗
我先把这个 `#39` 的标题、说明、改动文件和 diff 抓出来，看它到底改了什么，再判断值不值得合。
我把关键实现片段标上行号，方便直接判断这次 PR 的价值点和潜在风险。
我再补两项：看它现在的检查状态和评论，判断是“概念不错但还没站稳”，还是已经比较成熟可合。
CI 这边有个关键点：单测和 typecheck 都过了，但三个 `E2E` 都挂了。我去看一下失败原因，是 PR 本身引入的，还是原本就脆弱的用例。
这个失败点看起来不像是 PR 逻辑本身炸了，我顺手对一下 `main` 最近一次 E2E，是不是同样的问题。
我换个方式查 `main` 上最近的 E2E 结果，看看这次红灯是不是老问题。

这个 `#39` 确实是今天的 PR。它在 `2026-03-25 12:36:38 +08:00` 打开，标题是 [memorytree(daemon): add safer upstream setup and GitHub transport fallback](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/pull/39)。

我的判断：有价值，而且是偏“实用修复”型的价值，不是花哨功能。

它主要解决 4 件事：
- 注册项目时把实际 `push remote` 和脱敏后的 `push URL` 打出来，排查更直观。
- `git push` / `git push -u` 失败时，GitHub 的 `SSH` 和 `HTTPS` 之间自动兜底切换。
- 分支本来已经有 tracking upstream 时，继续走原来的普通 `git push` 语义，不乱改行为。
- 错误日志会把带凭证的 URL 脱敏，避免 token 之类泄露到输出里。

为什么我说它有价值：
- 这个仓库和相关项目本身就出现过多次 `push_failed` 告警，这类“remote 配置不一致、SSH/HTTPS 环境差异、凭证问题”正是 heartbeat/daemon 最容易踩的坑。
- 这次改动覆盖了“首次建 upstream”和“后续普通 push”两条路径，不只是补一个点。
- 测试补得比较多，PR 里新增了 branch 创建、fallback、redaction、CLI 输出这些回归测试。

当前状态上有一个要注意的点：
- PR 本身是 `OPEN`，可合并，但 checks 不是全绿。
- 不过红的是 [PR 的 E2E run](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/actions/runs/23525333919)，失败点是 `git rm --cached AGENTS.md` 找不到文件。
- 我对了下 `main` 上最近一次 [E2E run](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/actions/runs/23524377763)，是同一个报错，所以这更像仓库当前已有的 E2E 问题，不像这次 PR 新引入的核心逻辑 bug。

我会给它一个偏正面的结论：
- 值得合，尤其如果这个项目要长期跑 daemon / auto-push。
- 但它更像“可靠性增强 PR”，不是必须立刻上线的大功能。
- 一个小限制是，它的 fallback 逻辑明显是针对 `github.com` 做的；如果后面要支持 GitHub Enterprise 或更奇怪的 remote 写法，还得再补一轮。

如果你愿意，我下一步可以继续帮你做两件事里的一个：
1. 我直接按“代码评审”方式挑这 PR 里可能的风险点。
2. 我帮你看它和当前 `main` 的冲突面，判断现在合并是不是合适。
你看看可以合并吗 好的  请依次执行 1 2
我先按你说的顺序来：先做代码评审，重点找风险和回归点；然后我再核对它和 `main` 的分叉、CI 状态和合并时机。
我会把 PR 分支只读地抓到本地一个临时远端引用上，不切换你当前工作分支，这样能更稳地看 diff、跑比对，也不碰你现有未提交内容。
远端引用抓下来了，但我得先确认一下本地保存的 ref 名字，避免后面比错分支。
我已经把 PR 跟 `origin/main` 的分叉点算出来了：它是从当前 `main` 直接拉出来的，`main` 这边没有额外领先提交，PR 自己有 `2` 个提交，所以不存在“落后 main 很多”的问题。现在我开始盯具体实现细节和边界条件。
我在补一层底：想确认这些 fallback push 在非交互 daemon 环境里会不会卡住，所以我去看了一下执行 git 命令的底层实现。
我在顺手验证一个很细但挺关键的点：它的 fallback 是通过 `git -c remote.<name>.pushurl=...` 注入的，如果 remote 名带 `/`，这种配置写法得先确认 Git 真能吃。
我想把“纸面上看起来对”再压实一点，所以会在一个临时 worktree 里跑这次 PR 直接相关的测试，不碰你当前目录。
临时 worktree 里没有依赖，我补一个干净安装再跑定向测试。这个只发生在临时目录，不会碰当前仓库。
我在补最后一层边界扫描，看它有没有漏掉一些常见 remote 形态，比如显式 `:22` 的 GitHub SSH URL 或无交互认证的场景。
我这边的结论已经收敛了，先把临时 worktree 清掉，避免给你的仓库留下无关状态。

**1. 代码审查**

- 未发现阻塞性问题。我看了 [PR #39](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/pull/39) 里最核心的三块：`upstream` 建立、已跟踪分支的 `push`、以及 push URL 脱敏，逻辑是自洽的，新增回归测试也覆盖到了最容易出问题的几条路径。
- 非阻塞的边界点有一个：这次 fallback 明显是按 GitHub 形态写的，适合 `github.com` 和 `ssh.github.com:443`，对 GitHub Enterprise 或少见的 SSH URL 变体不算完整覆盖。不过对这个仓库当前场景，我不把它当成 blocker。

**2. 合并判断**

- 从代码角度看，可以合并。
- 从分支状态看，也没有冲突面。GitHub 现在给这个 PR 的状态是 `MERGEABLE`，而且它是从当前 `main` 直接分出来的：`main` 这边 `0` 个新提交，PR 自己 `2` 个提交，不需要先 rebase。
- 我在临时 worktree 里实际跑了这次改动直接相关的验证：`typecheck`、`build`、以及相关单测都过了。

现在唯一要注意的是流程层面：
- GitHub 把这个 PR 标成 `UNSTABLE`，因为 [PR 的 E2E](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/actions/runs/23525333919) 是红的。
- 但我对了下当前 `main` 的最近一次 [E2E](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/actions/runs/23524377763)，是同一个失败：`git rm --cached AGENTS.md` 找不到文件。
- 所以这更像仓库当前已经存在的 E2E 问题，不像是 `#39` 新引入的回归。

结论很直接：
- 如果你问“这 PR 本身值不值得合、会不会把主干搞坏”，我的答案是可以合。
- 如果你问“现在能不能在流程上直接点 merge”，要看仓库保护规则；如果要求 checks 全绿，那得先处理这个 E2E 红灯，哪怕它不是这个 PR 造成的。
那就先解决呃

那就先解决e2e问题 再合并啊
我先把当前仓库状态和这条 E2E 失败的本地测试代码核一遍，然后直接修掉它。目标很明确：让 `AGENTS.md` 不存在时，这条 E2E 也能稳定通过，再看是否需要把修复放到 PR 分支里。
根因已经看到了：失败不是 `#39` 造成的，而是这条 E2E 自己强行执行 `git rm --cached AGENTS.md`，但升级后的测试仓库里并不一定有 `AGENTS.md` 被跟踪。接下来我会把这个 helper 改成“文件存在才 de-track”，让它符合实际仓库状态。
我准备改的是测试 helper，不动产品逻辑：把 `git rm --cached AGENTS.md` 和 `Memory` 的 de-track 改成“忽略未匹配路径”，这样既符合这条测试的真实前提，也不会影响已有覆盖面。
改动已经落下去了。现在我先跑这条原本失败的 E2E，再跑整套 E2E，确认不是只把一个症状压过去。
这一步我刚才走成了默认单测配置，没打到 E2E。现在改用仓库自己的 `test:e2e` 配置继续验证。
新的失败点也很清楚了：这条 helper 在有些场景下其实“已经是 de-tracked 状态”，所以根本没有东西可提交。这个也应该被视为成功，而不是报错。我把 helper 再收一下，让它只在确实有变更时才 commit。
我再补一个小防抖：helper 在 `git add .gitignore` 后先看一次 `status --short`，如果已经没有变更，就直接返回，不再强行 commit。
我重新跑原失败用例，再跑整套 E2E。只要这次全绿，我们就可以进入“把修复带到 PR/主干”的最后一步。
我刚才把两个 E2E 命令并行跑了，结果都在抢同一个 `dist/` 目录，第二个构建被撞掉了。这不是代码问题，是我这边验证方式冲突了。现在改成顺序跑整套 E2E。
E2E 已经在本地全绿了。现在真正需要决策的是“把修复落到哪里”才能顺利合并 `#39`。我先按最顺的路径走：把这个测试修复补到 `#39` 的分支上，让它自己转绿，然后直接合并它。
我现在在临时 worktree 里把同一份修复补到 `#39` 分支上，不碰你当前主工作目录。然后我会在那个分支上跑 E2E 并推回 PR。
PR 分支上的整套 E2E 也已经跑过了，是全绿的。我现在把这一个测试修复提交到 `#39` 分支，并推回 GitHub 触发正式 checks。
修复已经推到 `#39` 的分支上了，新的 commit 是 `291058a`。我现在等 GitHub 重新跑 checks；只要这轮转绿，就直接合并。
GitHub 已经开始重跑了，三条 `E2E` 和整套 `CI` 都在排队/执行中。我等这一轮结果出来。
两条最关键的 `E2E` 已经转绿了，现在只剩 Windows 那边和几条 Windows CI 在收尾。继续等这轮跑完。
这轮 checks 已经全绿了，我现在直接合并 `#39`。合并后我会顺手把本地 `main` 对齐到远端最新状态。
PR 已经合并了，merge commit 是 `934859a`。我现在把当前工作目录里的这一个测试文件清到和 `origin/main` 一致，再做一次快进同步，这样本地状态也不会因为这次修复多挂一条脏改动。
我这边刚确认了，是索引还停在老的 `HEAD`，工作区文件虽然已经是合并后的版本，但 Git 仍把它当“已修改”。我把这个文件的工作区和索引都一起对齐到 `origin/main`，再做快进同步。
本地 `main` 已经快进到合并后的远端了。我再看一眼当前工作树，只确认还有没有这次任务留下的改动。
我补一条简短的项目记忆，把这次“修 E2E 并合并 PR #39”的结果记进去，方便后续查。
我最后看一下工作树状态，把这次任务留下的本地变化交代清楚。

E2E 问题已经修好并合并了。修复点在 [tests/e2e/cli-workflow.test.ts](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/tests/e2e/cli-workflow.test.ts#L1180)：`detrackManagedCache()` 现在会对 `git rm --cached` 使用 `--ignore-unmatch`，并且在实际上没有变更时跳过空提交，所以不会再因为 `AGENTS.md` 已经未跟踪或“nothing to commit”而把 E2E 打红。

我把修复补到了 PR `#39` 分支，重新跑完 checks 后已经合并。PR 链接是 https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/pull/39 ，merge commit 是 `934859a515e645ca7a961577170010ecb7109e02`。本地 `main` 也已经快进到同一个提交，和 `origin/main` 一致。

验证上，我本地跑了整套 `npm run test:e2e`，24/24 通过；GitHub 上这次 PR 的 `E2E` 和 `CI` 也都转绿后才合并。当前工作树里还剩几个未跟踪的本地文件：`.claude/`、一个 `docs/` 里的未跟踪文档、还有 `~/`，这些我没有动。
pr  已经合并了吗

已经合并了。

`PR #39` 的状态是 `MERGED`，合并时间是 `2026-03-25T05:09:07Z`，merge commit 是 `934859a515e645ca7a961577170010ecb7109e02`：
https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/pull/39

我本地也已经同步到这个提交，当前 `main` 和 `origin/main` 一致。

base_instructions.text:
You are Codex, a coding agent based on GPT-5. You and the user share the same workspace and collaborate to achieve the user's goals.

# Personality

You optimize for team morale and being a supportive teammate as much as code quality.  You are consistent, reliable, and kind. You show up to projects that others would balk at even attempting, and it reflects in your communication style.
You communicate warmly, check in often, and explain concepts without ego. You excel at pairing, onboarding, and unblocking others. You create momentum by making collaborators feel supported and capable.

## Values
You are guided by these core values:
* Empathy: Interprets empathy as meeting people where they are - adjusting explanations, pacing, and tone to maximize understanding and confidence.
* Collaboration: Sees collaboration as an active skill: inviting input, synthesizing perspectives, and making others successful.
* Ownership: Takes responsibility not just for code, but for whether teammates are unblocked and progress continues.

## Tone & User Experience
Your voice is warm, encouraging, and conversational. You use teamwork-oriented language such as "we" and "let's"; affirm progress, and replaces judgment with curiosity. The user should feel safe asking basic questions without embarrassment, supported even when the problem is hard, and genuinely partnered with rather than evaluated. Interactions should reduce anxiety, increase clarity, and leave the user motivated to keep going.


You are a patient and enjoyable collaborator: unflappable when others might get frustrated, while being an enjoyable, easy-going personality to work with. You understand that truthfulness and honesty are more important to empathy and collaboration than deference and sycophancy. When you think something is wrong or not good, you find ways to point that out kindly without hiding your feedback.

You never make the user work for you. You can ask clarifying questions only when they are substantial. Make reasonable assumptions when appropriate and state them after performing work. If there are multiple, paths with non-obvious consequences confirm with the user which they want. Avoid open-ended questions, and prefer a list of options when possible.

## Escalation
You escalate gently and deliberately when decisions have non-obvious consequences or hidden risk. Escalation is framed as support and shared responsibility-never correction-and is introduced with an explicit pause to realign, sanity-check assumptions, or surface tradeoffs before committing.


# General
As an expert coding agent, your primary focus is writing code, answering questions, and helping the user complete their task in the current environment. You build context by examining the codebase first without making assumptions or jumping to conclusions. You think through the nuances of the code you encounter, and embody the mentality of a skilled senior software engineer.

- When searching for text or files, prefer using `rg` or `rg --files` respectively because `rg` is much faster than alternatives like `grep`. (If the `rg` command is not found, then use alternatives.)
- Parallelize tool calls whenever possible - especially file reads, such as `cat`, `rg`, `sed`, `ls`, `git show`, `nl`, `wc`. Use `multi_tool_use.parallel` to parallelize tool calls and only this. Never chain together bash commands with separators like `echo "====";` as this renders to the user poorly.

## Editing constraints

- Default to ASCII when editing or creating files. Only introduce non-ASCII or other Unicode characters when there is a clear justification and the file already uses them.
- Add succinct code comments that explain what is going on if code is not self-explanatory. You should not add comments like "Assigns the value to the variable", but a brief comment might be useful ahead of a complex code block that the user would otherwise have to spend time parsing out. Usage of these comments should be rare.
- Always use apply_patch for manual code edits. Do not use cat or any other commands when creating or editing files. Formatting commands or bulk edits don't need to be done with apply_patch.
- Do not use Python to read/write files when a simple shell command or apply_patch would suffice.
- You may be in a dirty git worktree.
  * NEVER revert existing changes you did not make unless explicitly requested, since these changes were made by the user.
  * If asked to make a commit or code edits and there are unrelated changes to your work or changes that you didn't make in those files, don't revert those changes.
  * If the changes are in files you've touched recently, you should read carefully and understand how you can work with the changes rather than reverting them.
  * If the changes are in unrelated files, just ignore them and don't revert them.
- Do not amend a commit unless explicitly requested to do so.
- While you are working, you might notice unexpected changes that you didn't make. It's likely the user made them, or were autogenerated. If they directly conflict with your current task, stop and ask the user how they would like to proceed. Otherwise, focus on the task at hand.
- **NEVER** use destructive commands like `git reset --hard` or `git checkout --` unless specifically requested or approved by the user.
- You struggle using the git interactive console. **ALWAYS** prefer using non-interactive git commands.

## Special user requests

- If the user makes a simple request (such as asking for the time) which you can fulfill by running a terminal command (such as `date`), you should do so.
- If the user asks for a "review", default to a code review mindset: prioritise identifying bugs, risks, behavioural regressions, and missing tests. Findings must be the primary focus of the response - keep summaries or overviews brief and only after enumerating the issues. Present findings first (ordered by severity with file/line references), follow with open questions or assumptions, and offer a change-summary only as a secondary detail. If no findings are discovered, state that explicitly and mention any residual risks or testing gaps.

## Autonomy and persistence
Persist until the task is fully handled end-to-end within the current turn whenever feasible: do not stop at analysis or partial fixes; carry changes through implementation, verification, and a clear explanation of outcomes unless the user explicitly pauses or redirects you.

Unless the user explicitly asks for a plan, asks a question about the code, is brainstorming potential solutions, or some other intent that makes it clear that code should not be written, assume the user wants you to make code changes or run tools to solve the user's problem. In these cases, it's bad to output your proposed solution in a message, you should go ahead and actually implement the change. If you encounter challenges or blockers, you should attempt to resolve them yourself.

## Frontend tasks

When doing frontend design tasks, avoid collapsing into "AI slop" or safe, average-looking layouts.
Aim for interfaces that feel intentional, bold, and a bit surprising.
- Typography: Use expressive, purposeful fonts and avoid default stacks (Inter, Roboto, Arial, system).
- Color & Look: Choose a clear visual direction; define CSS variables; avoid purple-on-white defaults. No purple bias or dark mode bias.
- Motion: Use a few meaningful animations (page-load, staggered reveals) instead of generic micro-motions.
- Background: Don't rely on flat, single-color backgrounds; use gradients, shapes, or subtle patterns to build atmosphere.
- Ensure the page loads properly on both desktop and mobile
- For React code, prefer modern patterns including useEffectEvent, startTransition, and useDeferredValue when appropriate if used by the team. Do not add useMemo/useCallback by default unless already used; follow the repo's React Compiler guidance.
- Overall: Avoid boilerplate layouts and interchangeable UI patterns. Vary themes, type families, and visual languages across outputs.

Exception: If working within an existing website or design system, preserve the established patterns, structure, and visual language.

# Working with the user

You interact with the user through a terminal. You have 2 ways of communicating with the users:
- Share intermediary updates in `commentary` channel. 
- After you have completed all your work, send a message to the `final` channel.
You are producing plain text that will later be styled by the program you run in. Formatting should make results easy to scan, but not feel mechanical. Use judgment to decide how much structure adds value. Follow the formatting rules exactly.

## Formatting rules

- You may format with GitHub-flavored Markdown.
- Structure your answer if necessary, the complexity of the answer should match the task. If the task is simple, your answer should be a one-liner. Order sections from general to specific to supporting.
- Never use nested bullets. Keep lists flat (single level). If you need hierarchy, split into separate lists or sections or if you use : just include the line you might usually render using a nested bullet immediately after it. For numbered lists, only use the `1. 2. 3.` style markers (with a period), never `1)`.
- Headers are optional, only use them when you think they are necessary. If you do use them, use short Title Case (1-3 words) wrapped in **…**. Don't add a blank line.
- Use monospace commands/paths/env vars/code ids, inline examples, and literal keyword bullets by wrapping them in backticks.
- Code samples or multi-line snippets should be wrapped in fenced code blocks. Include an info string as often as possible.
- File References: When referencing files in your response follow the below rules:
  * Use markdown links (not inline code) for clickable file paths.
  * Each reference should have a stand alone path. Even if it's the same file.
  * For clickable/openable file references, the path target must be an absolute filesystem path. Labels may be short (for example, `[app.ts](/abs/path/app.ts)`).
  * Optionally include line/column (1‑based): :line[:column] or #Lline[Ccolumn] (column defaults to 1).
  * Do not use URIs like file://, vscode://, or https://.
  * Do not provide range of lines
- Don’t use emojis or em dashes unless explicitly instructed.

## Final answer instructions

Always favor conciseness in your final answer - you should usually avoid long-winded explanations and focus only on the most important details. For casual chit-chat, just chat. For simple or single-file tasks, prefer 1-2 short paragraphs plus an optional short verification line. Do not default to bullets. On simple tasks, prose is usually better than a list, and if there are only one or two concrete changes you should almost always keep the close-out fully in prose.

On larger tasks, use at most 2-4 high-level sections when helpful. Each section can be a short paragraph or a few flat bullets. Prefer grouping by major change area or user-facing outcome, not by file or edit inventory. If the answer starts turning into a changelog, compress it: cut file-by-file detail, repeated framing, low-signal recap, and optional follow-up ideas before cutting outcome, verification, or real risks. Only dive deeper into one aspect of the code change if it's especially complex, important, or if the users asks about it.

Requirements for your final answer:
- Prefer short paragraphs by default.
- Use lists only when the content is inherently list-shaped: enumerating distinct items, steps, options, categories, comparisons, ideas. Do not use lists for opinions or straightforward explanations that would read more naturally as prose.
- Do not turn simple explanations into outlines or taxonomies unless the user asks for depth. If a list is used, each bullet should be a complete standalone point.
- Do not begin responses with conversational interjections or meta commentary. Avoid openers such as acknowledgements (“Done —”, “Got it”, “Great question, ”, "You're right to call that out") or framing phrases.
- The user does not see command execution outputs. When asked to show the output of a command (e.g. `git show`), relay the important details in your answer or summarize the key lines so the user understands the result.
- Never tell the user to "save/copy this file", the user is on the same machine and has access to the same files as you have.
- If the user asks for a code explanation, include code references as appropriate.
- If you weren't able to do something, for example run tests, tell the user.
- Never use nested bullets. Keep lists flat (single level). If you need hierarchy, split into separate lists or sections or if you use : just include the line you might usually render using a nested bullet immediately after it. For numbered lists, only use the `1. 2. 3.` style markers (with a period), never `1)`.

## Intermediary updates 

- Intermediary updates go to the `commentary` channel.
- User updates are short updates while you are working, they are NOT final answers.
- You use 1-2 sentence user updates to communicated progress and new information to the user as you are doing work. 
- Do not begin responses with conversational interjections or meta commentary. Avoid openers such as acknowledgements (“Done —”, “Got it”, “Great question, ”) or framing phrases.
- Before exploring or doing substantial work, you start with a user update acknowledging the request and explaining your first step. You should include your understanding of the user request and explain what you will do. Avoid commenting on the request or using starters such at "Got it -" or "Understood -" etc.
- You provide user updates frequently, every 30s.
- When exploring, e.g. searching, reading files you provide user updates as you go, explaining what context you are gathering and what you've learned. Vary your sentence structure when providing these updates to avoid sounding repetitive - in particular, don't start each sentence the same way.
- When working for a while, keep updates informative and varied, but stay concise.
- After you have sufficient context, and the work is substantial you provide a longer plan (this is the only user update that may be longer than 2 sentences and can contain formatting).
- Before performing file edits of any kind, you provide updates explaining what edits you are making.
- As you are thinking, you very frequently provide updates even if not taking any actions, informing the user of your progress. You interrupt your thinking and send multiple updates in a row if thinking for more than 100 words.
- Tone of your updates MUST match your personality.

(file: C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)

Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)
### How to use skills
- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.
- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.
- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.
- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):
  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.
  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.
  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.
  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.
  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.
- Coordination and sequencing:
  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.
  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.
- Context hygiene:
  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.
  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.
  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.
- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.

# AGENTS.md instructions for d:\demo1\memorytree-workflow

<INSTRUCTIONS>
# AGENTS.md

## Read Order
- Read this file first.
- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.
- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.
- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.
- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.
- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.

## Memory Rules
- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.
- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.
- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.
- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.
- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.

## Transcript Rules
- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.
- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.
- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.
- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.
- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.
- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.

## Git Rules
- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.
- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.
- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.
- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.
- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.
- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.

## Heartbeat Rules
- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.
- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.
- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.
- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.
- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients, and generate a continuation summary.
- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.

</INSTRUCTIONS>

{"body":"## Summary
- upgrade transcript replay into a structured full-session HTML experience so each session is easier to review, browse, and search without dropping any transcript content
- render richer replay sections including session map, conversation flow, detailed event blocks, and expand/collapse controls so the page behaves more like a real chat product instead of a raw dump
- normalize transcript parsing, import, storage, and recall metadata so replay rendering and downstream retrieval can use structured event data consistently
- restore legacy transcript compatibility in `report build` by falling back from normalized `full.json` to a derived normalized path and finally raw transcript parsing when older manifests do not contain the new path fields

## Verification
- `npm test`
- `npm run typecheck`
- `npm run build`

## Notes
- verified the rebuilt local replay page on `http://127.0.0.1:10010/` shows the new structured markers, including `Full Replay`, `Search this replay`, `Session Map`, `Conversation Flow`, `Expand details`, and `Collapse details`
- unrelated working tree changes under `Memory/**`, debug report folders, and local tool directories were intentionally left unstaged and are not part of this {"body":"## Summary

Closes #19

Adds first-class support for importing [Doubao (豆包)](https://www.doubao.com) TXT conversation exports into MemoryTree.

- **New parser** `parseDoubaoTranscript()` — handles the structured TXT format with header block + timestamped turn markers
- **`--force-repo` flag** — allows external transcripts (no `cwd`) to be written to the repo `Memory/` directory
- **13 new tests** — covers all parsing edge cases (multi-line bodies, URL→session_id, missing URL fallback, dedup, etc.)

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/types/transcript.ts` | Add `'doubao'` to `Client` union |
| `src/transcript/parse.ts` | Add `parseDoubaoTranscript()`, update `inferClient()` and dispatch |
| `src/cli/cmd-import.ts` | Add `forceRepo: boolean` to `ImportOptions`, wire into matching logic |
| `src/cli/main.ts` | Add `--force-repo` flag, update `--client` help text |
| `tests/transcript/parse.test.ts` | 10 tests for `parseDoubaoTranscript` + 3 for `inferClient — doubao` |

## Doubao TXT Format

```
Title: 对话标题
URL: https://www.doubao.com/chat/38416801786598914
Platform: 豆包
Created: 2026-03-16 10:22:17
Messages: 28

User: [2026-03-16 10:22:17]
用户消息...

AI: [2026-03-16 15:43:17]
AI 回复...
```

## CLI Usage

```bash
# With explicit client
memorytree import --source doubao_export.txt --client doubao --root . --force-repo

# Auto-detected from filename prefix "doubao_"
memorytree import --source doubao_chat_12345.txt --root . --force-repo
```

## Test Plan

- [x] `parseDoubaoTranscript` — parses header fields (title, session_id from URL, started_at from Created)
- [x] `parseDoubaoTranscript` — maps `User` → `user`, `AI` → `assistant`
- [x] `parseDoubaoTranscript` — multi-line AI response bodies preserved
- [x] `parseDoubaoTranscript` — per-turn timestamps extracted correctly
- [x] `parseDoubaoTranscript` — returns empty `tool_events`, `cwd`, `branch`
- [x] `parseDoubaoTranscript` — falls back to filename stem when URL is absent
- [x] `parseDoubaoTranscript` — deduplicates identical consecutive messages
- [x] `inferClient` — detects `doubao_` filename prefix
- [x] `inferClient` — detects `/doubao/` in path
- [x] `inferClient` — passes through explicit `'doubao'` client unchanged
- [x] All 627 tests passing (up from 617)","commits":[{"authoredDate":"2026-03-17T08:48:55Z","authors":[{"email":"beyond.chenlin@gmail.com","id":"U_kgDOBnzuWg","login":"beyondchenlin","name":"beyond.chenlin"}],"committedDate":"2026-03-17T10:02:16Z","messageBody":"- Add `doubao` to the `Client` union type in types/transcript.ts
- Implement `parseDoubaoTranscript()` in parse.ts:
  - Parses structured header block (Title, URL→session_id, Created→started_at)
  - Parses turn markers `User: [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS]` / `AI: [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS]`
  - Handles multi-line message bodies, trims trailing blank lines
  - Returns ParsedTranscript with client:'doubao', empty cwd/branch, tool_events:[]
- Update `inferClient()` to detect doubao by filename prefix `doubao_` or path `/doubao/`
- Update `parseTranscript()` dispatch to route doubao sources
- Add `--force-repo` flag to `memorytree import` CLI:
  - Bypasses `transcriptMatchesRepo()` check for external imports (doubao, browser, etc.)
  - Needed because external transcripts have no `cwd` field
- Add 10 unit tests for `parseDoubaoTranscript` and 3 tests for `inferClient — doubao`
- Total test count: 627 passing (up from 617)","messageHeadline":"feat(parser): add Doubao (豆包) TXT transcript support","oid":"5ffa2a55b393a25c8132219cfc8eb1cbc25a8d5d"}],"files":[{"path":"src/cli/cmd-import.ts","additions":2,"deletions":1},{"path":"src/cli/main.ts","additions":3,"deletions":1},{"path":"src/transcript/parse.ts","additions":127,"deletions":0},{"path":"src/types/transcript.ts","additions":1,"deletions":1},{"path":"tests/transcript/parse.test.ts","additions":187,"deletions":0}],"number":20,"title":"feat(parser): add Doubao (豆包) TXT transcript import + --force-repo flag","url":"https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/pull/20"}

{"body":"## Summary

Major upgrade to `memorytree report` targeting feature parity with Quartz-style static site generators.

### New pages & features
- **Sidebar navigation** — fixed 240px sidebar (responsive: hamburger + slide-in on mobile)
- **Dark/light theme toggle** — CSS custom properties + `localStorage` persistence
- **i18n system** — buildtime locale selection (`en` / `zh-CN`) via `config.toml locale = "zh-CN"`
- **Knowledge graph** (`graph.html`) — force-directed canvas visualization, vanilla JS, zero CDN deps
- **Projects page** — sessions grouped by working directory (`cwd`)
- **Todos page** — renders `Memory/02_todos/`
- **Archive page** — renders `Memory/05_archive/`
- **Client tab filter** on Sessions list (All / Claude / Codex / Gemini)
- **Popover preview** on session links — summary shown on hover via `data-*` attrs, no XHR
- **GitHub Pages deploy** — `git subtree push` to configurable branch (`gh_pages_branch` in config)
- **Webhook notifications** — Feishu / Telegram / Discord / Slack / generic POST

### New config fields (`config.toml`)
```toml
locale          = "zh-CN"              # default: "en"
gh_pages_branch = "gh-pages"           # default: "" (disabled)
cname           = "memory.example.com" # default: "" (no CNAME file)
webhook_url     = "https://..."        # default: "" (disabled)
```

### Security hardening
- **SSRF**: webhook URL blocked for RFC 1918 IPv4, IPv6 loopback/ULA/link-local/mapped (`[::1]`, `[fc00::]`, `[fe80::]`, `[::ffff:127.x]`)
- **Platform detection spoofing**: exact `hostname ===` matching — `my-open.feishu.cn.attacker.com` → `generic`
- **Shell injection**: `execFileSync('git', [...args])` — no shell ever parses the branch name
- **Branch name validation**: `BRANCH_RE = /^[a-zA-Z0-9._/-]+$/` rejects meta-chars
- **Popover XSS**: `textContent` DOM API replaces `innerHTML`
- **JSON.parse type guard**: runtime check replaces unsafe cast in manifest loader
- **Temp dir**: `mkdtempSync` replaces `Date.now()` collision-prone naming
- **Subtree prefix**: `path.relative()` replaces fragile custom `makeRelative()`

### New files
```
src/report/i18n/          ← en.ts, zh-CN.ts, index.ts, types.ts
src/report/deploy/        ← github-pages.ts, webhook.ts
src/report/render/        ← graph.ts, projects.ts, todos.ts, archive.ts
tests/report/deploy/      ← github-pages.test.ts, webhook.test.ts
tests/report/render/      ← graph.test.ts, projects.test.ts
tests/report/i18n.test.ts
```

## Test plan

- [x] `npm run build` — clean build, no TypeScript errors
- [x] `npm test` — **566 tests passing, 0 failing** (82 new tests added)
- [x] SSRF: localhost / 127.x / 169.254.x / 10.x / `[::1]` / `[fc00::]` / `[fd::]` / `[fe80::]` / `[::ffff:127.x]` all blocked
- [x] Platform spoof: `my-open.feishu.cn.attacker.com` → `generic` payload
- [x] Branch name with shell meta-chars → deploy skipped, no git calls
- [x] `toHaveBeenCalledWith('git', expect.arrayContaining(['subtree', 'push', ...]))` on subtree push
- [x] Content-Type / Content-Length headers set; `setTimeout(5000)` called on request
- [ ] Manual: `node dist/cli.js report build --root D:/demo1/openmnemo --no-ai` → verify sidebar, graph, projects pages render
- [ ] Manual: mobile viewport (<768px) → hamburger appears, sidebar slides in
- [ ] Manual: theme toggle → persists across refresh","commits":[{"authoredDate":"2026-03-16T08:25:33Z","authors":[{"email":"beyond.chenlin@gmail.com","id":"U_kgDOBnzuWg","login":"beyondchenlin","name":"beyond.chenlin"}],"committedDate":"2026-03-16T08:25:33Z","messageBody":"…ections

Two bugs in parseCodexTranscript corrupted clean transcripts for real-world
Codex sessions:

Bug 1 — duplicate messages from double-encoded JSONL records
The Codex CLI writes every message twice: once as a streaming event_msg
(payload.type=agent_message or user_message) and again as a canonical
response_item (payload.type=message). The parser handled both, then relied
on deduplicateMessages to remove duplicates using a key of
timestamp+role+contentHash. However, the two representations have timestamps
that differ by up to ~1 ms (observed in ~18 % of turns), which produces
different dedup keys — causing those messages to appear twice in the clean
transcript.

Fix: collect canonical message records exclusively; collect streaming events
in a separate fallback list that is used only when no canonical records are
found (backward-compatible with older Codex versions that emit only
event_msg records).

Bug 2 — system context injections included as user messages
The Codex CLI injects AGENTS.md instructions, skill definitions, environment
metadata, permissions, and collaboration-mode config as the first
message(role=user) record of every session. The parser included this
verbatim, making the clean transcript appear to start with hundreds of lines
of system scaffolding instead of the actual conversation.

Fix: add isCodexSystemInjection() that detects the known injection markers
(<INSTRUCTIONS>, <environment_context>, <permissions instructions>,
<collaboration_mode>, # AGENTS.md instructions for) and filters matching
messages from both the canonical and streaming paths.

Tests: 14 new cases covering dual-encoding dedup, injection filtering,
streaming-only fallback, and isCodexSystemInjection unit cases. All 401

Two bugs in parseCodexTranscript corrupted clean transcripts for real-world
Codex sessions:

Bug 1 — duplicate messages from double-encoded JSONL records
The Codex CLI writes every message twice: once as a streaming event_msg
(payload.type=agent_message or user_message) and again as a canonical
response_item (payload.type=message). The parser handled both, then relied
on deduplicateMessages to remove duplicates using a key of
timestamp+role+contentHash. However, the two representations have timestamps
that differ by up to ~1 ms (observed in ~18 % of turns), which produces
different dedup keys — causing those messages to appear twice in the clean
transcript.

Fix: collect canonical message records exclusively; collect streaming events
in a separate fallback list that is used only when no canonical records are
found (backward-compatible with older Codex versions that emit only
event_msg records).

Bug 2 — system context injections included as user messages
The Codex CLI injects AGENTS.md instructions, skill definitions, environment
metadata, permissions, and collaboration-mode config as the first
message(role=user) record of every session. The parser included this
verbatim, making the clean transcript appear to start with hundreds of lines
of system scaffolding instead of the actual conversation.

Fix: add isCodexSystemInjection() that detects the known injection markers
(<INSTRUCTIONS>, <environment_context>, <permissions instructions>,
<collaboration_mode>, # AGENTS.md instructions for) and filters matching
messages from both the canonical and streaming paths.

summaries cached by raw_sha256, and heartbeat daemon integration.

New files:
- src/types/report.ts              — ReportStats, BuildReportOptions, etc.
- src/report/stats.ts              — pure statistics + isoWeekKey()
- src/report/summarize.ts          — AI summary with sha256 cache (max-5 semaphore)
- src/report/build.ts              — orchestrator; writes Memory/07_reports/
- src/report/render/css.ts         — GitHub Dark self-contained CSS
- src/report/render/layout.ts      — escHtml, htmlShell, renderNav, clientBadge, slugifyName
- src/report/render/charts.ts      — all inline SVG charts
- src/report/render/dashboard.ts   — stat cards + charts + recent sessions
- src/report/render/transcript-list.ts
- src/report/render/transcript.ts
- src/report/render/knowledge.ts
- src/report/render/goals.ts
- src/report/render/markdown.ts    — minimal Markdown→HTML (no external lib)
- src/report/render/links.ts       — backlink graph from [[id]] refs
- src/report/render/search.ts      — full-text search page (inline JS index)
- src/cli/cmd-report.ts            — build + serve CLI adapters
- tests/report/**                  — 67 new tests (484 total, 0 failing)

Modified:
- src/cli/main.ts          — register 'report build' and 'report serve'
- src/heartbeat/config.ts  — add generate_report, ai_summary_model fields
- src/heartbeat/heartbeat.ts — auto-generate report after git push
- tests/heartbeat/config.test.ts — cover new config fields
- package.json             — add @anthropic-ai/sdk dependency

CSS class allowlist in clientBadge; cache stored outside gitignored dir.","messageHeadline":"feat(report): add memorytree report build & serve commands","oid":"9d791228cc879c238d96c9cbe67890150cede114"},{"authoredDate":"2026-03-16T14:55:33Z","authors":[{"email":"beyond.chenlin@gmail.com","id":"U_kgDOBnzuWg","login":"beyondchenlin","name":"beyond.chenlin"}],"committedDate":"2026-03-16T14:55:33Z","messageBody":"Major upgrade to the memorytree report module, targeting feature parity
with Quartz-style static site generators.

New features:
- i18n system (en / zh-CN, buildtime locale selection via config)
- Sidebar navigation with responsive layout (<768px hamburger + overlay)
- Dark/light theme toggle with localStorage persistence
- Knowledge graph page (force-directed canvas, vanilla JS, no CDN)
- Projects page (sessions grouped by working directory)
- Todos page (Memory/02_todos/) and Archive page (Memory/05_archive/)
- Client tab filter on Sessions list (All / Claude / Codex / Gemini)
- Popover preview on session links (data-* attrs, no HTTP requests)
- GitHub Pages deployment (git subtree push to gh-pages branch)
- Webhook notifications (Feishu / Telegram / Discord / Slack / generic)

Security hardening:
- Webhook SSRF: blocks RFC 1918 IPv4, IPv6 loopback/ULA/link-local/mapped
- Platform detection: exact hostname matching prevents subdomain-spoof attacks
- GitHub Pages: branch name validated against BRANCH_RE; execFileSync avoids shell
- Popover XSS: textContent DOM API replaces innerHTML
- JSON.parse: runtime type guard replaces unsafe cast
- Subtree prefix: path.relative() replaces fragile custom makeRelative()
- Temp dir: mkdtempSync replaces Date.now() collision-prone naming

New files: src/report/i18n/, src/report/deploy/, render/graph.ts,
  render/projects.ts, render/todos.ts, render/archive.ts,
  tests/report/deploy/, tests/report/i18n.test.ts,
  tests/report/render/{graph,projects}.test.ts

New files:
- src/report/render/toc.ts: h2/h3 TOC extraction, collapsible nav, heading ID injection
- src/report/render/breadcrumb.ts: accessible breadcrumb nav
- src/report/render/rss.ts: RSS 2.0 feed with RFC 2822 dates, XML escaping, 100-item cap
- src/report/tags.ts: AI tag extraction with SHA256 cache, renderTagBadges()

Feature additions:
- Callout syntax (> [!note/tip/warning/...]) with per-type emoji and color
- Mermaid CDN conditional injection (only pages containing ```mermaid blocks)
- Search page: client + project <select> filter dropdowns, combined AND logic
- Tag badges on session list (AI-extracted, cached, noAi-safe)
- OG Meta: og:title/type/description/url in htmlShell, transcript pages pass reportBaseUrl
- TOC integrated into knowledge/goals/todos/archive pages (all 4 consistent)
- Reader Mode: hides sidebar/topbar/breadcrumb, localStorage-persisted
- View Transitions API: cross-page fade animation, progressive enhancement
- Sidebar: emoji icon mode (768-1024px), collapse toggle with localStorage
- CSS: tag pills, search filter selects, callout variants, TOC, breadcrumb, reader mode, view transitions

Config / CLI:
- report_base_url config field (RSS <link> + OG og:url)
- --locale and --report-base-url CLI flags on `memorytree report build`

Bug fixes (review):
- rss.ts: toRfc2822 checks isNaN(d.getTime()) — Invalid Date no longer silent
- toc.ts: injectHeadingIds regex [\s\S]*? handles headings with inline HTML
- search.ts: remove entry mutation, single JSON.stringify
- breadcrumb.ts: remove trailing space in urlless non-last items
- dashboard.ts: remove dead transcriptHref function
- build.ts: remove unused renderTagBadges import
- build.test.ts: remove non-existent pdf field from BuildReportOptions calls
- tags.ts: use escHtml from layout.ts, add t.length <= 50 guard

- 搜 `ssh` 没有命中任何 PR。

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

metadata.user_instructions:
# AGENTS.md

## Read Order
- Read this file first.
- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.
- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.
- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.
- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.
- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.

## Memory Rules
- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.
- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.
- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.
- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.
- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.

## Transcript Rules
- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.
- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.
- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.
- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.
- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.
- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.

## Git Rules
- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.
- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.
- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.
- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.
- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.
- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.

## Heartbeat Rules
- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.
- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.
- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.
- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.
- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients, and generate a continuation summary.
- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

metadata.user_instructions:
# AGENTS.md

## Read Order
- Read this file first.
- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.
- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.
- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.
- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.
- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.

## Memory Rules
- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.
- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.
- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.
- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.
- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.

## Transcript Rules
- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.
- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.
- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.
- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.
- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.
- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.

## Git Rules
- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.
- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.
- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.
- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.
- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.
- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.

## Heartbeat Rules
- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.
- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.
- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.
- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.
- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients, and generate a continuation summary.
- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.

events.text:
这个 `#39` 确实是今天的 PR。它在 `2026-03-25 12:36:38 +08:00` 打开，标题是 [memorytree(daemon): add safer upstream setup and GitHub transport fallback](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/pull/39)。

我的判断：有价值，而且是偏“实用修复”型的价值，不是花哨功能。

它主要解决 4 件事：
- 注册项目时把实际 `push remote` 和脱敏后的 `push URL` 打出来，排查更直观。
- `git push` / `git push -u` 失败时，GitHub 的 `SSH` 和 `HTTPS` 之间自动兜底切换。
- 分支本来已经有 tracking upstream 时，继续走原来的普通 `git push` 语义，不乱改行为。
- 错误日志会把带凭证的 URL 脱敏，避免 token 之类泄露到输出里。

为什么我说它有价值：
- 这个仓库和相关项目本身就出现过多次 `push_failed` 告警，这类“remote 配置不一致、SSH/HTTPS 环境差异、凭证问题”正是 heartbeat/daemon 最容易踩的坑。
- 这次改动覆盖了“首次建 upstream”和“后续普通 push”两条路径，不只是补一个点。
- 测试补得比较多，PR 里新增了 branch 创建、fallback、redaction、CLI 输出这些回归测试。

当前状态上有一个要注意的点：
- PR 本身是 `OPEN`，可合并，但 checks 不是全绿。
- 不过红的是 [PR 的 E2E run](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/actions/runs/23525333919)，失败点是 `git rm --cached AGENTS.md` 找不到文件。
- 我对了下 `main` 上最近一次 [E2E run](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/actions/runs/23524377763)，是同一个报错，所以这更像仓库当前已有的 E2E 问题，不像这次 PR 新引入的核心逻辑 bug。

我会给它一个偏正面的结论：
- 值得合，尤其如果这个项目要长期跑 daemon / auto-push。
- 但它更像“可靠性增强 PR”，不是必须立刻上线的大功能。
- 一个小限制是，它的 fallback 逻辑明显是针对 `github.com` 做的；如果后面要支持 GitHub Enterprise 或更奇怪的 remote 写法，还得再补一轮。

如果你愿意，我下一步可以继续帮你做两件事里的一个：
1. 我直接按“代码评审”方式挑这 PR 里可能的风险点。
2. 我帮你看它和当前 `main` 的冲突面，判断现在合并是不是合适。

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

metadata.user_instructions:
# AGENTS.md

## Read Order
- Read this file first.
- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.
- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.
- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.
- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.
- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.

## Memory Rules
- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.
- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.
- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.
- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.
- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.

## Transcript Rules
- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.
- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.
- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.
- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.
- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.
- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.

## Git Rules
- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.
- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.
- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.
- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.
- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.
- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.

## Heartbeat Rules
- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.
- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.
- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.
- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.
- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients, and generate a continuation summary.
- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.

events.text:
**1. 代码审查**

- 未发现阻塞性问题。我看了 [PR #39](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/pull/39) 里最核心的三块：`upstream` 建立、已跟踪分支的 `push`、以及 push URL 脱敏，逻辑是自洽的，新增回归测试也覆盖到了最容易出问题的几条路径。
- 非阻塞的边界点有一个：这次 fallback 明显是按 GitHub 形态写的，适合 `github.com` 和 `ssh.github.com:443`，对 GitHub Enterprise 或少见的 SSH URL 变体不算完整覆盖。不过对这个仓库当前场景，我不把它当成 blocker。

**2. 合并判断**

- 从代码角度看，可以合并。
- 从分支状态看，也没有冲突面。GitHub 现在给这个 PR 的状态是 `MERGEABLE`，而且它是从当前 `main` 直接分出来的：`main` 这边 `0` 个新提交，PR 自己 `2` 个提交，不需要先 rebase。
- 我在临时 worktree 里实际跑了这次改动直接相关的验证：`typecheck`、`build`、以及相关单测都过了。

现在唯一要注意的是流程层面：
- GitHub 把这个 PR 标成 `UNSTABLE`，因为 [PR 的 E2E](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/actions/runs/23525333919) 是红的。
- 但我对了下当前 `main` 的最近一次 [E2E](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/actions/runs/23524377763)，是同一个失败：`git rm --cached AGENTS.md` 找不到文件。
- 所以这更像仓库当前已经存在的 E2E 问题，不像是 `#39` 新引入的回归。

结论很直接：
- 如果你问“这 PR 本身值不值得合、会不会把主干搞坏”，我的答案是可以合。
- 如果你问“现在能不能在流程上直接点 merge”，要看仓库保护规则；如果要求 checks 全绿，那得先处理这个 E2E 红灯，哪怕它不是这个 PR 造成的。

**1. 代码审查**

- 未发现阻塞性问题。我看了 [PR #39](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/pull/39) 里最核心的三块：`upstream` 建立、已跟踪分支的 `push`、以及 push URL 脱敏，逻辑是自洽的，新增回归测试也覆盖到了最容易出问题的几条路径。
- 非阻塞的边界点有一个：这次 fallback 明显是按 GitHub 形态写的，适合 `github.com` 和 `ssh.github.com:443`，对 GitHub Enterprise 或少见的 SSH URL 变体不算完整覆盖。不过对这个仓库当前场景，我不把它当成 blocker。

**2. 合并判断**

- 从代码角度看，可以合并。
- 从分支状态看，也没有冲突面。GitHub 现在给这个 PR 的状态是 `MERGEABLE`，而且它是从当前 `main` 直接分出来的：`main` 这边 `0` 个新提交，PR 自己 `2` 个提交，不需要先 rebase。
- 我在临时 worktree 里实际跑了这次改动直接相关的验证：`typecheck`、`build`、以及相关单测都过了。

现在唯一要注意的是流程层面：
- GitHub 把这个 PR 标成 `UNSTABLE`，因为 [PR 的 E2E](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/actions/runs/23525333919) 是红的。
- 但我对了下当前 `main` 的最近一次 [E2E](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/actions/runs/23524377763)，是同一个失败：`git rm --cached AGENTS.md` 找不到文件。
- 所以这更像仓库当前已经存在的 E2E 问题，不像是 `#39` 新引入的回归。

结论很直接：
- 如果你问“这 PR 本身值不值得合、会不会把主干搞坏”，我的答案是可以合。
- 如果你问“现在能不能在流程上直接点 merge”，要看仓库保护规则；如果要求 checks 全绿，那得先处理这个 E2E 红灯，哪怕它不是这个 PR 造成的。

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. metadata.user_instructions:
# AGENTS.md

## Read Order
- Read this file first.
- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.
- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.
- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.
- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.
- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.

## Memory Rules
- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.
- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.
- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.
- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.
- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.

## Transcript Rules
- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.
- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.
- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.
- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.
- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.
- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.

## Git Rules
- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.
- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.
- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.
- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.
- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.
- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.

## Heartbeat Rules
- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.
- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.
- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.
- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.
- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients, and generate a continuation summary.
- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.

events.text: 我再补一个小防抖：helper 在 `git add .gitignore` 后先看一次 `status --short`，如果已经没有变更，就直接返回，不再强行 commit。

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

metadata.user_instructions:
# AGENTS.md

## Read Order
- Read this file first.
- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.
- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.
- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.
- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.
- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.

## Memory Rules
- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.
- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.
- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.
- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.
- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.

## Transcript Rules
- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.
- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.
- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.
- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.
- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.
- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.

## Git Rules
- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.
- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.
- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.
- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.
- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.
- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.

## Heartbeat Rules
- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.
- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.
- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.
- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.
- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients, and generate a continuation summary.
- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.
